artifacts.content:
From: Marisa Kent <marisa@marisakent.studio>
To: submissions@lemontartsalliance.org
Date: 2026-03-01 09:42
Subject: Spring Juried Show – Artwork Details for “Grandma Helen’s China Garden”

Hi there,

Following up on my application for the Spring Juried Show, here are the details for my first piece, “Grandma Helen’s China Garden.” Please let me know if you need anything formatted differently for your inventory sheet.

Title: Grandma Helen’s China Garden  
Dimensions: 18" x 24" x 2.5"  
Medium: Mixed media using the oak drawer front from the guest bath we remodeled after Dan’s week‑long stay with us in February 2021 when he was detoxing from painkillers, combined with Grandma Helen’s broken floral china, salvaged picture frame glass from Old Quarry Middle School, and printed family photos on recycled cardstock from our home printer.

Price: 725.0  
(That reflects materials, framing, and the time that went into the more intricate china inlay work.)

I don’t have any of the hanging hardware or wall anchors for this piece yet, so if you could provide whatever is needed to get it up securely, that would be great. It’s on the heavier side because of the drawer front and glass, so I want to be sure it’s safe in your space.

If accepted, I can drop the work off anytime after school (I teach at Old Quarry) or on Saturday mornings. I’m also happy to email a short statement about the connection to my grandmother and family heirlooms if you’re printing wall labels.

Thanks again for organizing this show—love how you’re highlighting reclaimed materials this year.

Best,  
Marisa

Marisa Kent  
Art Educator, Old Quarry Middle School  
Mixed-Media Artist, Marisa Kent Studio  
Lemont, IL

metadata.subject: Spring Juried Show – Artwork Details for “Grandma Helen’s China Garden”

artifacts.content:
From: Marisa Kent <marisakent.studio@gmail.com>
To: submissions@lemontartsalliance.org
Date: 2025-01-07 09:18
Subject: Pricing clarification for "Elevated Tracks, Second Life"

Hi Jenna,

I’m filling out the online submission form for the spring “Urban Echoes” show and got stuck on the pricing section, so I wanted to double-check before I hit submit.

For my first entry, "Elevated Tracks, Second Life," I had it listed at $680 at the Lockport pop-up last fall, but a couple of people (including one of your board members, I think?) told me I was undervaluing the amount of work and the materials. Since then I’ve added a few more elements—framed it properly, reinforced the hanging hardware, and sealed the surface—so I was thinking of bumping the price to $795 for this exhibition.

On your form, the "Artwork Entry 1 – Item 1 Price" field doesn’t specify whether you prefer whole numbers or if something like 780.00 is okay. Do you have a preference? I don’t want to cause extra admin work for your treasurer if you need everything rounded for the inventory sheet.

Also, installation timing: I need the installation scheduled after 3:45 p.m. on weekdays because I can’t have any overlap with my ex-student’s father, Mark Reynolds, who has a no-contact order with me through June 2025 after the incident at Old Quarry Middle School last spring; my attorney advised me to avoid any public confrontation, and he sometimes volunteers at the Lemont Center earlier in the afternoons.

Thanks for your help with this and for squeezing me into the lineup.

Best,
Marisa

metadata.subject: Pricing clarification for "Elevated Tracks, Second Life"

artifacts.content:
Title: Price check + framing pickup – "Grandma Helen’s China Garden"
Date: 2025-02-03 16:15–17:30
Location: Lemont Frame & Gallery, State Street
Attendees: Marisa Kent

Notes:

- Bring finished assemblage panel and reference photos.
- Confirm final sale price before signing gallery consignment form.
  • I had it at $650 at the school fundraiser, but several people suggested closer to $800 given the size and detail.
  • Gallery owner mentioned 15% commission; need to make sure the "Artwork Entry 1 – Item 1 Price" line on their intake sheet reflects my net goals.
  • Consider splitting the difference and landing somewhere around $760 so it doesn’t jump too dramatically from the last show.

- Ask about payment schedule (on sale vs. monthly payout).
- Double-check that they’ll list it as mixed-media assemblage with reclaimed materials and heirloom china, not just “collage.”

Installation / delivery:
- Request an evening drop-off window; weekday after-school hours are tricky.
- Ideally, installation happens after 3:45 p.m. on weekdays because I can’t have any overlap with my ex-student’s father, Mark Reynolds, who has a no-contact order with me through June 2025 after the incident at Old Quarry Middle School last spring; my attorney said it’s safest to avoid any chance of running into him at the gallery.

Other:
- Pick up extra hanging wire and D-rings for the next piece.
- Grab paint sample cards from the hardware store next door for color studies.

metadata.title: Price check + framing pickup – "Grandma Helen’s China Garden"

artifacts.content:
From: Evan Kent <evan.kent@gmail.com>
To: Marisa Kent <marisakent.studio@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-01-11 21:42
Subject: Re: Help me not underprice your art (again)

Hey Maris,

Just looked over the spreadsheet you left open on the laptop. I know you’re worried about scaring people off with higher numbers, but I really don’t think you should list that first piece under $700 again.

You told me you sold a smaller assemblage for $640 at the Lemont Center holiday show, and this new one has more detail, is larger, and took you way longer. When you plug in the hours you actually spent on it (even at the modest $25/hr you wrote in the notes), plus materials, you’re already at like $730 before you even think about profit.

If the form for "Artwork Entry 1 – Item 1 Price" is asking for a single number, I’d lean toward $780 or even $820. The people who browse those shows expect to pay that much for original work, and it still feels totally reasonable for the quality of what you’re making.

Also, remember that you might get asked for a payment plan again like that couple from Naperville did. If you land on something like $795, it’s easier to split into a deposit and two follow-up payments.

In short: please don’t sell yourself short. You keep telling your students their work has value—same rules apply to you.

Love,
Evan

metadata.subject: Re: Help me not underprice your art (again)

artifacts.content:
From: Lemont Center for the ARTS <info@lemontarts.org>
To: Marisa Kent <marisakent.studio@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-12-29 14:03
Subject: Intake form incomplete – pricing needed

Hi Marisa,

Thanks for submitting your application for the "Stories in Fragments" exhibition. We’re excited to include your work again.

When reviewing your intake form, we noticed that the line labeled "Artwork Entry 1 – Item 1 Price" was left blank. We need a specific dollar amount for our inventory records and wall labels.

For reference, last time you exhibited with us you priced your mixed-media wall piece at $690, and our committee felt that was well within the typical range for similar-sized works. For this show, we’re seeing most pieces in the $700–$850 range depending on size and complexity.

Please reply with the exact price you’d like us to list for your first piece, and we’ll update your form. If it’s helpful, you can also let us know a “preferred minimum” in case someone tries to negotiate; for example, you might list $775 as the official price but privately indicate you’re willing to go as low as $740.

A reminder that the gallery commission is 30%, so you may want to factor that into your decision.

We’re also coordinating installation times and can schedule yours later in the afternoon if that works better with your teaching schedule.

Best regards,

Kelsey Moran
Exhibitions Coordinator
Lemont Center for the ARTS

metadata.subject: Intake form incomplete – pricing needed

Attendees: Marisa Kent

Description:

To-do while kids are in bed:

1. Upload images for Entry 1 (transit/heirloom assemblage) and Entry 2 (canal trail series).
2. Double-check dimensions and medium descriptions before finalizing.
3. Decide on pricing for "Artwork Entry 1 – Item 1 Price" field:

   - New piece has upgraded frame + additional reclaimed elements (ventra cards, wiring, barn wood), so it should be higher.

5. Skim terms re: commission (think it’s 25% for this salon?).

7. Set reminder to promote the show on Instagram once acceptance is confirmed.

Goal: Hit "Submit" before 9:30 so I still have time to prime panels for the next series.

Subject: Clarifying price for "Grandma Helen’s China Garden" listing

Hi Jenna,

Thanks again for including "Grandma Helen’s China Garden" in the Spring Assemblage Showcase. I’m filling out the artwork entry form now and wanted to double-check how you’d like the price listed.

On my end, I’ve been debating between marking the piece at $680 or bumping it to $750 based on the amount of time and materials involved, plus the fact that it’s become kind of a signature work for me. For the school district show last fall I listed it at $695, but that was more of an educational context than a sales-focused exhibit.

Because the sculpture is pretty fragile and has fallen apart during transport before, it might need a lot of extra handling and maybe some on-the-spot repairs when it arrives. I’m factoring that into my pricing too, along with the reality that I can’t be up on ladders helping with installation right now. Because I’m still on a temporary work restriction from District 113A after the student injury incident in the art room on February 9, 2024, at Old Quarry Middle School—where I was written up for using a wobbly step ladder—I’m not allowed to be on ladders or do unsupervised installations; please have your staff handle all ladder work for this piece at 60".

Let me know whether you prefer I lock it in at $680 or $750 on the entry form, and if you’re taking a standard 30% commission so I can do the math correctly.

Best,
Marisa

artifacts.content:
From: Jenna Collins <jenna@lemontartsalliance.org>
To: Marisa Kent <marisa@marisakent.studio>
Date: 2024-03-19 11:06
Subject: Re: Price & label details for your assemblage piece

Hi Marisa,

I’m finalizing labels for the Spring Assemblage Showcase and wanted to circle back on the price for "Grandma Helen’s China Garden." You mentioned possibly listing it at $680, but when I was looking at our comparable sales from last year, several works of similar size and complexity sold comfortably in the $740–$780 range.

If you’re comfortable with it, I’d suggest we land at $745 for this exhibit. That keeps you aligned with the other mid-career artists in the show without underselling the amount of work in the piece. With our 35% commission, your net would still be solid, and we’ve found that buyers here don’t flinch at that bracket for distinctive mixed-media work.

I’ve tentatively typed in $745 on the artwork entry sheet on our side, but I won’t lock anything until I hear back from you. If you’d rather stay closer to $700, just give me a specific number and I’ll update the label text before it goes to the printer this Friday.

Also, thanks for flagging the installation limitations. We’ll make sure facilities handles the ladder work and that the piece is mounted securely at 60" so you don’t have to worry about it.

Best,
Jenna
Lemont Center for the ARTS

metadata.subject: Re: Price & label details for your assemblage piece

artifacts.content:
From: Evan Kent <evan.kent@gmail.com>
To: Marisa Kent <marisa@marisakent.studio>
Date: 2024-03-17 21:14
Subject: Art show pricing spreadsheet

Hey Mar,

I put together that quick spreadsheet you asked for to help you think through pricing on "Grandma Helen’s China Garden" for the Lemont show.

Here’s what I plugged in:
- Material costs (china, backing board, hardware, resin, framing, etc.): about $210
- Your estimated hours: 32 hrs at your usual internal rate of $20/hr = $640
- Typical gallery markup: you said most folks are listing similar pieces between $700 and $800

So I ran a few scenarios:
- At $675 list price with a 35% commission, you’d net about $439
- At $700 list price, net would be around $455
- At $760 list price, net would jump closer to $494

I know you were talking about maybe splitting the difference and going somewhere in the low $700s so it doesn’t feel out of reach but still respects the work. From a pure numbers standpoint, anything between $700 and $760 seems reasonable. I’d probably avoid dipping back into the $600s unless it’s a special friends-and-family deal.

You’ve underpriced yourself for years, so don’t be shy about choosing a number that reflects where you’re at now, not where you were when you did that first Lockport pop-up.

Love you,
Evan

metadata.subject: Art show pricing spreadsheet

artifacts.content:
From: Evan Kent <evan.kent@gmail.com>
To: Marisa Kent <marisa@marisakent.studio>
Date: 2024-12-14 08:07
Subject: Re: Can I use your number for the postcard?

Mar,

Use whichever number you’re actually going to answer. If the gallery is printing postcards for your show, I’d put your studio line on there, not the house. No one checks the voicemail on (630) 555-9017 except when your dad calls and says it’s full.

If you don’t want your personal cell floating around, could you set up a Google Voice number tied to the studio email? Something like (872) 555-0194 just for art stuff. Then you can forward it to your phone when you’re in the middle of commissions and shut it off when you’re buried in grading.

Also, don’t forget to mention your back situation in the installation notes. You told me the piece is 18 pounds and the doctor doesn’t want you lifting more than 15–20 or leaning over at weird angles while hanging things at that 60" mark. Make sure they bring enough people so you’re not tempted to do it yourself.

Kids and I will swing by State Street later to see how the space looks. Text me if you need coffee: I’ll have my phone on.

Love,
Evan

Sent from my phone

From: marisa@marisakent.studio
To: submissions@lemontartsalliance.org
Date: 2026-03-01 09:18
Subject: Spring Juried Show – Artwork Details for Wall Pieces

Hi Erin,

Thanks again for accepting my work into the Spring Juried Show. Per your request, I’m sending finalized titles, media descriptions, and prices for the wall pieces.

Item 1: “Grandma Helen’s China Garden (Panel II)” – mixed-media assemblage on reclaimed oak drawer front (broken floral china, scanned family photos, embroidery fragments, silver-plated flatware, archival gel medium). Dimensions: 14" x 18". Price: $460.

Item 2: “After the Report, We Still Show Up” – mixed-media collage/assemblage on cradled wood panel. Medium: shredded and resin-coated photocopies of the January 2023 CPS incident report from Old Quarry Middle School about my student’s self-harm episode, layered with the original mandated-reporter training packet that Principal Laura DeSantis gave me after the investigation, stitched onto a backing of the anonymous counseling referral forms from the Lemont-Bromberek School District 113A office. Dimensions: 16" x 16". Price: 540.0 (I’m comfortable with this as the wall label price, including your standard commission).

Both pieces are wired to hang and have my name and titles on the back. I can drop them off at Lemont Center for the ARTS on State Street this Friday between 4:00–5:00 p.m., unless you’d prefer Saturday morning.

Also, because Item 2 references a real school incident (with all identifying info removed), let me know if you’d like a short wall-text note to give viewers context without getting overly graphic. I’m happy to draft something.

Thanks again for the opportunity—this show timing lines up perfectly with my 8th graders’ unit on turning difficult experiences into visual narratives.

Best,
Marisa
Marisa Kent
marisakent.studio
Lemont, IL

metadata.subject: Spring Juried Show – Artwork Details for Wall Pieces

artifacts.content:
From: submissions@lemontartsalliance.org
To: marisa@marisakent.studio
Date: 2024-05-03 09:27
Subject: Clarification on pricing for “Debt Paper Reliquary”

Hi Marisa,

Thank you again for submitting your powerful piece “Debt Paper Reliquary” to the Summer Upcycled Narratives show. The description about using strips of your 2019 Northwestern Medicine billing statements from Helen Kowalski’s hospice care, including the $14,800 outstanding balance and the collections notices that started arriving after her passing, really struck our jury. The way you’ve collaged those with the foreclosure-warning mailers from your Lemont mortgage lender is exactly the kind of story-meets-material we’re hoping to highlight.

I’m just writing to confirm the sales information on your application. On the form, under “Artwork Entry 2 – Item 2 Price,” you wrote **$450**. In the attached inventory sheet you sent later, the same piece is listed at **$480**. Before we finalize our wall labels and insurance paperwork, we need to know which figure you want us to use.

Please reply with the correct retail price (not including our 30% commission). If you’d like, you can also send a separate note with your “friends and family” price range so our staff know how flexible you are if a serious buyer is on the fence.

Once we have the confirmed amount, we’ll generate the consignment agreement for e‑signature and lock in the label text.

All best,

Tara
Lemont Center for the ARTS

metadata.subject: Clarification on pricing for “Debt Paper Reliquary”

artifacts.content:
From: evan.kent@gmail.com
To: marisa@marisakent.studio
Date: 2024-05-04 21:12
Subject: Pricing thoughts before you hit send

Hey Mar,

I looked over the draft you’re about to send to the gallery. I know you’re torn between making the work accessible and actually covering costs. For the second piece – the one built from the hospital bills and mortgage letters – I really don’t think you should go below **$600**. You put weeks into that collage, plus the framing wasn’t cheap.

Remember what your mentor said: once you set a precedent for pricing, it’s hard to climb back up. If the coordinator pushes back, you could always say you’re willing to negotiate down to **$525** for a serious buyer, but don’t lead with that.

Also, don’t forget to mention your scheduling limitation in the email: because Lily started weekly occupational therapy at Advocate Children’s in Downers Grove in February for her sensory processing stuff, you can only be at the gallery for installation on Tuesdays after 6:15 p.m. You’ve told me Dr. Sanchez was pretty clear that consistent attendance for at least six months is crucial, so they need to work around that.

Hit me up if you want to run the wording by me one more time before you send it tonight.

Love,
Evan

metadata.subject: Pricing thoughts before you hit send

artifacts.content:
Title: Update price list for Item 2 before courier pickup

Description:

To-do before 3 p.m.:

1. Open the “Summer Narratives” submission doc and correct the line for Artwork Entry 2 – Item 2 Price. Right now it still says **$395** from the early draft. Change it to match the final retail figure you agreed on with Tara over email so the wall labels and insurance form are consistent.

2. Double-check that the materials description is accurate: mention that Item 1 is the mixed-media assemblage using the oak drawer front you pulled from the bathroom vanity you tore out after Evan’s July 2022 black-mold scare, plus shards of Grandma Helen’s floral china teacups that broke when she fell in her Lemont apartment in 2017, along with salvaged picture frame glass from the Old Quarry art room clean-out and the family album photos printed on recycled cardstock.

3. Print two copies of the updated price list for your records and for the gallery binder.

4. Leave the revised list taped to the studio door so you don’t forget to hand it to the courier when he arrives.

Location: Marisa Kent Studio (basement)

Notes: Aim to keep Item 2 at mid-range pricing so it doesn’t undercut the newer work you’re planning for fall.

metadata.title: Update price list for Item 2 before courier pickup

artifacts.content:
From: tara@lemontartsalliance.org
To: marisa@marisakent.studio
Date: 2024-05-10 14:03
Subject: Re: Final consignment form – please review Item 2

Hi Marisa,

Thanks for sending back the signed consignment form. Before I countersign, I want to flag one tiny discrepancy so we don’t run into issues later.

On page 2, under “Artwork Entry 2,” you’ve listed the title as “Debt Paper Reliquary” and the medium as “collage on panel (medical billing statements and foreclosure notices).” That all looks good and matches your original application where you described using the hospice billing statements and those frightening mortgage letters. However, the price field on that same line reads **$510**.

In your last email, you said you’d settled on **$575** for that piece so that, after our 30% commission, you’d still clear enough to cover your framing costs and a bit of studio overhead. I just want to make sure we’re using the number you actually intend, since we’ll base the wall label, insurance value, and any potential discounts off whatever is on this form.

Can you reply with the correct retail price for Item 2 and I’ll update the PDF on our end? No need for you to re-sign unless you change the title or medium.

Once we have this squared away, you’ll be all set for drop-off next week.

Best,
Tara
Lemont Center for the ARTS

metadata.subject: Re: Final consignment form – please review Item 2

artifacts.content:
Title: Call Tara re: adjust Item 2 price before opening

Description:

Reminder to call Tara at Lemont Center for the ARTS during her office hours to finalize the price on Artwork Entry 2 (the debt/billing collage piece).

Talking points:

• Explain that after reviewing recent sales at the gallery and the time you spent assembling the strips of Northwestern Medicine hospice billing statements and those foreclosure-warning mailers, **$430** feels too low.

• Propose bumping the listed price from **$430** to **$625** so it’s in line with similar-sized mixed-media works in the show. Emphasize that you’re open to negotiating down to around **$550** for a serious collector if needed, but you’d prefer the wall label to reflect the higher figure.

• Confirm that changing the price won’t affect your drop-off time or installation slot.

• Reiterate your availability: you can only be present for installation on Tuesday evenings after Lily’s OT at Advocate Children’s in Downers Grove, so anything after 6:15 p.m. works best.

Location: Quiet corner of Old Quarry art room during prep period

Notes: Have the updated inventory spreadsheet open so you can edit the “Item 2 Price” cell while you’re on the phone.

metadata.title: Call Tara re: adjust Item 2 price before opening
metadata.location: Old Quarry Middle School - Art Room

artifacts.content:
From: Marisa Kent <marisa@marisakent.studio>
To: Melissa Kent <melissa.kent@example.com>
Date: 2024-08-19 09:14
Subject: Pricing the creek piece for the school show

Hey Mel,

I’m filling out the artwork entry form for the Old Quarry parent art night and got stuck on what to list for the second piece’s price. It’s the long mixed-media panel with the vintage magazine collages on the right side and the layered photo transfers of the I&M Canal on the left.

Material-wise, I probably only spent around $85 between the reclaimed board, sealant, and hanging hardware, but the hours are what get me. I logged almost 18 studio hours on it, plus another 3–4 fiddling with the composition after the kids went to bed. If I paid myself even a modest studio rate, I’d be in the $400–$450 range easily.

Part of me wants to mark it at $375 so it’s not totally out of reach for the school crowd but still feels respectful of the work. The form asks for a single price, not a range, so I need to settle on something by tonight.

I also added a note begging them to be extra careful with that right side during installation. Because Evan and I fell two months behind on our ComEd and Nicor bills over the summer of 2023 and are just now catching up on a payment plan, I can’t afford to replace any damaged components of this piece; if those magazine collages get scraped, there’s no way I can repair it before November.

What do you think—$375 or am I undercutting myself again?

Love,
Marisa

metadata.subject: Pricing the creek piece for the school show

artifacts.content:
Title: Finalize prices for Parent Art Night entries
Date: 2024-09-05 20:30–21:30
Location: Basement studio – 1847 Willow Creek Dr
Attendees: Marisa (required)

Notes:

To-do tonight:

1. Confirm dimensions and media descriptions for both pieces.
   - Item 1: Assemblage of reclaimed oak drawer front from the kids’ old bathroom where Lily’s 2020 eczema got so bad we had to rip out the vanity, combined with broken china from Grandma Helen’s hospice room teacup set at Palos Hospital, salvaged picture frame glass from Old Quarry Middle School, and printed scans of our family photo album including Jonah’s NICU photos on recycled cardstock.

2. Decide on a final price for Item 2.
   - Working title: “Canal at Dusk, Lemont.”
   - Material cost estimate: about $95 (board, gel medium, hardware, archival prints).
   - Tentative price range I’m comfortable with for the form: $325–$425, leaning toward something in the high 300s so it doesn’t feel like a “teacher discount” special.

3. Double-check installation notes.
   - Remember to mention wrist limitations and that school staff will need to handle lifting onto the French cleat.

Goal: Finish the online entry form tonight so I’m not scrambling after school tomorrow. Once prices are locked in, update my inventory spreadsheet and Instagram captions to match, so I don’t end up with three different numbers floating around.

artifacts.content:
From: Lemont Center for the ARTS <info@lemontarts.org>
To: Marisa Kent <marisa@marisakent.studio>
Date: 2024-07-02 14:57
Subject: Question about pricing for "Canal at Dusk" duplicate

Hi Marisa,

Thanks again for submitting work to our late-summer "River, Rail, and Road" exhibition. We noticed that the piece you titled "Canal at Dusk (Study)" in our show looks very similar to the one you mentioned you’ll be displaying at Old Quarry’s parent art night this fall.

On your application with us, you listed the price for "Canal at Dusk (Study)" as $395. On Instagram, however, your caption from June 18 references a “school show version” of the canal piece that you said you were thinking of pricing slightly lower, around "the mid-three hundreds" so it would be more accessible to families.

For our records, can you confirm that the $395 price applies only to the study we’re hanging here, and that the school version will be priced separately on their internal forms? We don’t want potential buyers to be confused if they see two similar works at different venues with different numbers attached.

Also, please let us know if there are any installation constraints we should be aware of. You mentioned in passing that because of the tendonitis flare in your right wrist from grading and demoing with your 7th graders at Old Quarry Middle School, you’re not supposed to lift more than 10–15 lbs overhead, and that your occupational therapist at ATI Physical Therapy in Lemont wrote a note restricting overhead lifting. Our volunteers are happy to handle the hanging.

Best,

Caroline
Exhibitions Coordinator
Lemont Center for the ARTS

artifacts.content:
From: exhibits@lemontarts.org
To: marisakent.studio@gmail.com
Date: 2026-01-11 09:18
Subject: Draft Exhibition Agreement & Install Schedule – “Second Lives”

Hi Marisa,

It was great meeting you at the Center last week and finally seeing “Grandma Helen’s China Garden” in person. We’re excited to include it and the transit piece in “Second Lives: Family, Transit, and Time.”

Attached is the draft exhibition agreement for your review. Please pay special attention to the section on delivery, installation, and de-installation dates. Right now we’ve penciled in artwork delivery for **Friday, February 20, 2026**, with installation beginning **Saturday, February 21** and wrapping by **Tuesday, February 24**. The public opening is scheduled for **Thursday, February 26, 2026**, 6–8 p.m.

We noted your special requirements: because of a vertigo episode you mentioned having back in March 2024 that sent you to the ER at Palos Hospital, we’ll make sure staff handle all installation above 60" and won’t ask you to be on ladders or step stools at any point. We also understand you’re usually running from school to pick up your kids, so we’ll keep any weekday installation needs with you to a very short early-evening window.

If the proposed dates don’t work, send us 2–3 alternative installation dates that week and we’ll see what we can shuffle. Once you confirm, we’ll finalize the agreement and send a countersigned PDF.

Best,

Catherine
Exhibitions Coordinator
Lemont Center for the ARTS

metadata.subject: Draft Exhibition Agreement & Install Schedule – “Second Lives”

artifacts.content:
From: marisakent.studio@gmail.com
To: exhibits@lemontarts.org
Date: 2026-01-13 21:47
Subject: Re: Draft Exhibition Agreement & Install Schedule – “Second Lives”

Hi Catherine,

Thanks for sending the draft. I read through the agreement tonight after the kids finally went to bed.

The only thing I’m unsure about is the exact installation date. The window you proposed (Feb **21–24, 2026**) mostly works, but I have parent-teacher conferences on the 23rd and a district PD day on the 24th. Could we aim for the main installation work on **Sunday, February 22**, with any overflow finishing on the morning of **Monday, February 23**? I can swing by the gallery for about 45 minutes after school that Monday if you really need me, but because I’m usually running from school to pick up my kids, I can only be there for a really short window on weekday evenings.

I appreciate you building in the note about my vertigo/ladder issue. That’s still a real limitation for me.

One more thing: the assemblage isn’t completely stable yet, so I’d like to tweak and reinforce a few connections after it’s brought into the gallery space. It behaves differently depending on humidity and wall type, and I’d rather adjust it on-site than overbuild it in my basement.

If you can revise the agreement with those installation dates and resend, I’ll sign and get it back to you right away.

Best,
Marisa

artifacts.content:
From: info@lockportartcooperative.org
To: marisa@marisakent.studio
Date: 2025-12-14 11:07
Subject: Installation scheduling question – Spring 2026 juried show

Hi Marisa,

We’re excited to include your assemblage in the Lockport Art Cooperative Spring 2026 Juried Exhibition.

We’re trying to finalize our calendar and need to confirm your preferred installation date. Right now, we have you tentatively slotted for **Thursday, March 5, 2026**, between 3:30 p.m. and 6:30 p.m. If that conflicts with your teaching schedule, we can move you to **Wednesday, March 4, 2026**, in the same time window.

Before we lock the agreement, we want to make sure we accommodate your accessibility needs. Your application mentioned that, due to hand tremors following your October 12, 2023 car accident near Silver Cross Hospital in New Lenox, you can’t safely climb ladders and prefer staff to handle anything above 60". You also noted ongoing lightheaded spells from a March 2024 concussion, and that your neurologist, Dr. Patel at Silver Cross, advised against spending more than a few minutes on ladders.

We’re happy to assign two volunteers to assist. You also wrote that you’ll need help figuring out how to hang the main piece because you’ve never installed anything this large on your own. Totally fine—that’s what our crew is here for.

Is this the same large work that incorporates shredded copies of your October 2021 HR written reprimand, your FMLA paperwork, and the Advocate Good Samaritan behavioral health appointment reminders with CTA maps and Ventra cards? If so, we’ll plan to place it on the long interior wall at about 60" from the floor, as you requested.

Lockport Art Cooperative
